meanings.usage: 一种原产于美洲的大型猫科动物，可数名词
尤指偶然看到不易察觉的事物，常用搭配：spot sb/sth doing sth
表示具体的位置或物体上的点状痕迹
可作及物动词表示“积累某物”，也可作不及物动词表示“逐渐增多”
常用被动结构：be/feel obliged to do sth 表示“觉得有义务做某事”
用于修饰形容词或副词，加强程度
常用搭配：hunt for sb/sth 表示“搜寻某人/某物”
可作及物或不及物动词
后可接名词、that从句或wh-从句，表示确认某件事的真实性
常用被动语态be cornered表示“被逼入绝境”
指空间的角落或道路的拐角处
指人或动物经过时留下的一系列痕迹，常用搭配：a trail of sth
不及物动词，过去式和过去分词为clung，常用搭配：cling to sth
常用结构：convince sb of sth / convince sb that 从句，被动结构be convinced that表示“坚信…”
常用搭配：in the possession of sb 表示“为某人所有”，in possession of 表示“某人拥有某物”

meanings.meaning: 令人不安的；引起恐慌的
meanings.usage: 用于修饰事物，区别于disturbed（感到不安的，修饰人）

phrases.usage: 表示（危险的人或动物）在逃、逍遥法外；也可表示“总的来说”

examples.en: The escaped prisoner is still at large.
examples.zh: 逃犯仍然在逃。

examples.en: People at large are against the new policy.
examples.zh: 总的来说人们反对这项新政策。

phrases.usage: 认真对待某事，重视某事，否定形式为not take sth seriously

examples.en: You should take your parents' advice seriously.
examples.zh: 你应该认真对待父母的建议。

examples.en: His threat was not taken seriously at first.
examples.zh: 一开始没人把他的威胁当回事。

phrases.usage: 觉得有义务做某事，被迫做某事，强调主观上的责任感

examples.en: I felt obliged to help him when he was in trouble.
examples.zh: 他遇到困难时我觉得有义务帮他。

examples.en: The company felt obliged to apologize for the mistake.
examples.zh: 公司觉得有必要为这个错误道歉。

phrases.usage: 留下；遗留，宾语可放在leave和behind之间，也可放在behind之后

examples.en: The thief left behind a wallet at the scene of the crime.
examples.zh: 小偷在犯罪现场留下了一个钱包。

examples.en: Don't leave your belongings behind when you get off the bus.
examples.zh: 下车时别落下你的随身物品。

phrases.usage: 抱怨；诉说（病痛、不适等），后接名词或动名词

examples.en: Many residents complain of the noise from the construction site.
examples.zh: 很多居民抱怨工地的噪音。

examples.en: The patient complained of having a headache for three days.
examples.zh: 病人诉说自己已经头疼三天了。

phrases.usage: 为某人所有，主语是被拥有的事物，注意和in possession of（主语是拥有者）区分

examples.en: The rare painting is now in the possession of a private collector.
examples.zh: 这幅罕见的画作现在为一位私人收藏家所有。

examples.en: The company is in possession of three advanced production lines.
examples.zh: 这家公司拥有三条先进的生产线。

phrases.usage: 持续；继续，此处表示事件持续发生，后可接一段时间

examples.en: The meeting went on for more than three hours.
examples.zh: 会议持续了三个多小时。

examples.en: The rain has been going on since this morning.
examples.zh: 雨从今天早上就一直下个不停。

grammar.title: 情态动词表推测（对过去的肯定推测）
grammar.definition: 用来表示对过去已经发生的事情的确定性推断，说话人对推测的内容有较高的把握
grammar.structure: must have done / must have been doing
grammar.usage: 仅用于肯定句中，否定推测用can't have done，疑问推测用can have done

examples.en: This one must have been in the possession of a private collector and somehow managed to escape.
examples.zh: 这只美洲狮肯定是某个私人收藏家的，不知怎么逃出来了。

examples.en: The ground is wet. It must have rained last night.
examples.zh: 地面是湿的，昨晚肯定下雨了。

examples.en: He knew everything about the plan. He must have attended the meeting yesterday.
examples.zh: 他对这个计划了如指掌，他昨天肯定参加了会议。

grammar.title: 同位语从句
grammar.definition: 在复合句中用作同位语的从句，用于解释说明前面名词的具体内容
grammar.structure: 抽象名词（news, fact, report, idea, thought等）+ that + 完整陈述语序的从句
grammar.usage: that在同位语从句中不充当任何成分，仅起连接作用，无实际含义，一般不可省略

examples.en: When reports came into London Zoo that a wild puma had been spotted forty-five miles south of London, they were not taken seriously.
examples.zh: 当伦敦动物园接到报告说，在伦敦以南45英里处发现了一只野生美洲狮时，这些报告并没有受到重视。

examples.en: The news that our team won the game excited all of us.
examples.zh: 我们队赢了比赛的消息让我们所有人都很兴奋。

examples.en: There is no doubt that he is the right person for the job.
examples.zh: 毫无疑问他是这份工作的合适人选。

grammar.title: 让步/原因状语从句的省略
grammar.definition: 当状语从句的主语和主句主语一致，且从句谓语含有be动词时，可以省略从句的主语和be动词
grammar.structure: 连词（when, while, if, though等）+ 现在分词/过去分词/介词短语/形容词
grammar.usage: 现在分词表示和主语是主动关系，过去分词表示和主语是被动关系

examples.en: The hunt for the puma began in a small village where a woman picking blackberries saw 'a large cat' only five yards away from her.
examples.zh: 搜寻美洲狮的工作是从一个小村庄开始的，那里有一位采黑莓的妇女看见“一只大猫”离她只有五码远。（此处picking blackberries是定语从句who was picking blackberries的省略）

examples.en: When crossing the street, you should look both sides.
examples.zh: 过马路的时候你应该两边都看看。（省略了you are）

examples.en: If given more time, I will finish the task better.
examples.zh: 如果给我更多时间，我会把任务完成得更好。（省略了I am）

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is + 形容词 + to think that...

original.en: It is disturbing to think that a dangerous wild animal is still at large in the quiet countryside.
original.zh: 想到在宁静的乡村里有一头危险的野兽仍然在逃，就令人不安。

imitations.en: It is exciting to think that we will go to the beach for vacation next week.
imitations.zh: 想到我们下周要去海边度假，就很令人兴奋。

imitations.en: It is disappointing to think that he failed the exam again after studying so hard.
imitations.zh: 想到他这么努力学习还是考试不及格，就很令人失望。

sentencePatterns.pattern: However, as sth began to accumulate, sb felt obliged to do sth, for...

original.en: However, as the evidence began to accumulate, experts from the Zoo felt obliged to investigate, for the descriptions given by people who claimed to have seen the puma were extraordinarily similar.
original.zh: 然而，随着证据越来越多，动物园的专家们感到有必要进行调查，因为凡是声称见到过美洲狮的人们所描述的情况出奇地相似。

imitations.en: However, as the complaints from customers began to accumulate, the company felt obliged to recall the product, for the design flaw had caused several safety accidents.
imitations.zh: 然而，随着消费者的投诉越来越多，公司感到有必要召回产品，因为这个设计缺陷已经造成了多起安全事故。

imitations.en: However, as the waste in the river began to accumulate, the local residents felt obliged to report to the environmental department, for the water had become too dirty to use.
imitations.zh: 然而，随着河里的垃圾越积越多，当地居民觉得有必要向环保部门举报，因为水已经脏得没法用了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: The search/problem proved + 形容词, for...

original.en: The search proved difficult, for the puma was often observed at one place in the morning and at another place twenty miles away in the evening.
original.zh: 搜寻工作很困难，因为常常是早晨在甲地发现那只美洲狮，晚上却在20英里外的乙地出现。

imitations.en: The project proved challenging, for we lacked the professional technology and enough funding.
imitations.zh: 这个项目很有挑战性，因为我们缺少专业技术和足够的资金。

imitations.en: The final exam proved easy, for all the questions were from the exercises we had done before.
imitations.zh: 期末考试很简单，因为所有题目都来自我们之前做过的练习。
